rulings.Case Title: HAFI S MUHANNAD M.D VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petitioners Petition For Administrative Writ
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2019, Line 1, (tentative ruling part 1 of 2) PETITIONER HAFI MUHANNAD'S Notice Of Hearing On Petitioners Petition For Administrative Writ Petitioner Muhannad S. Hafi, M.D.'s petition for administrative writ of mandate is denied. *The Courts full tenative ruling has been emailed to all counsel on June 18, 2019 ** Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: HAFI S MUHANNAD M.D VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Hafi Muhannad Notice Of Hearing On Petitioners Petition For Administrative Writ (Tenative Ruling Only Part 2 Of 2)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2019, Line 1, Petitioner Hafi Muhannad's Notice Of Hearing On Petitioners Petition For Administrative Writ (Tenative Ruling Only Part 2 Of 2) Finally, Petitioner's claim that his conduct with Patient 2 "does not meet the requirements of sexual contact as set forth in Section 729, because there is no allegation that Petitioner had sexual intercourse or touched an 'intimate part' of a patient for the purpose of sexual arousal, gratification, or abuse" (Memo. at 6-7) is mistaken. Section 729 defines sexual exploitation by a physician as engaging in "an act of . . . sexual contact with a patient." (Bus. & Prof. Code § 729(a).) Sexual contact, in turn, is defined to mean "sexual intercourse or the touching of an intimate part of a patient for the purpose of sexual arousal, gratification, or abuse." (Id. § 729(c)(3).) The terms "intimate part" and "touching" have the same meanings as in Penal Code Section 243.4. (Id. § 729(c)(4).) That section defines an intimate part to include "the breast of a female" and touching to include "physical contact with another person, whether accomplished directly, through the clothing of the person committing the offense, or through the clothing of the victim." (Pen. Code § 243.4(f),(g)(1).) Here, the ALJ found that Petitioner pressed his stethoscope into patient 2's breasts, an "intimate part" of patient 2, while aroused and pressing his erect penis against her knees, and that he later pressed his erect penis against patient 2's thigh/hip area. (Dec. and Order at 7.) The finding of a violation is amply supported by the evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Board is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative (end of tenative ruling part 2 of 2) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2019, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT PACIFIC AUXILIARY FIRE ALARM COMPANY DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-Defendant Pacific Auxiliary Fire Alarm Company's demurrer to the first and fourth causes of Patrick McNerney's cross-complaint for express and implied contractual indemnity and breach of contract is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The cross-complaint fails adequately to plead the specific written or oral contract(s) on which the cross-claims are based. (See Harris v. Rudin, Richman & Appel (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 299, 307 ["if the action is based on alleged breach of a written contract, the terms must be set out verbatim in the body of the complaint or a copy of the written agreement must be attached and incorporated by reference."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for PAFA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ERIKA MCCARTNEY VS. FOODS ALIVE INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ERIKA MCCARTNEY'S Motion And Motion For Entry Of Consent Judgment The motion of Plaintiff Erika McCartney for entry of consent judgment is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The Court will sign the proposed order and consent judgment included with the moving papers. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BRETT TODOROFF VS. JOE QUINN BRETZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiffs For Order Imposing Sanctions Of $5,00.00 Against Attorney David P. Nemecek, Jr., Of The Fortress Law Firm, Inc., For Motion For Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS BRETT TODOROFF, MARK FINLEY'S Motion By Plaintiffs For Order Imposing Sanctions Of $5,00.00 Against Attorney David P. Nemecek, Jr., Of The Fortress Law Firm, Inc., For Motion For Judgment Plaintiffs Brett Todoroff and Mark Finley's motion for sanctions against attorney David P. Nemecek, Jr. is denied without prejudice to their renewing the motion before the assigned trial judge. Plaintiffs seek sanctions against Defendants' counsel for filing what they contend was a repetitive and duplicative motion for judgment on the pleadings. On May 20, 2019, this Court took that matter off calendar due to the parties' failure to meet and confer in person or by telephone regarding the basis for the motion as required by Code of Civil Procedure Section 439(a). Defendants' counsel apparently has refiled the motion on behalf of Defendant Randolph Bretz to be heard at the outset of trial, which is scheduled for Monday, June 17, 2019. Plaintiffs may renew their request for sanctions before the trial court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JASON ROUSSOS VS. POUR GUYS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Pour Guys Soma Llc Dba Tempest Bar & Grill'S Motion To Modify The Scope Of A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 19, 2019, Line 4, Defendant Pour Guys Soma Llc Dba Tempest Bar & Grill'S Motion To Modify The Scope Of A Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Counsel to the prevailing party shall submit a copy of the proposed order prior to the hearing via email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WAYNE LOK ET AL VS. YI QUN LI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 19, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF WAYNE LOK, RAYMOND LOK's MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' unopposed Motion to Compel is GRANTED. Defendants shall provide code-compliant verified responses and responsive documents to plaintiff's counsel within one week of notice of this order (notice to be served by overnight mail). If plaintiff fails to provide code-compliant verified responses and responsive documents to plaintiff's counsel within one week of notice of this order, sanctions in the amount of $2,000 in favor of plaintiffs and against defendants and their counsel of record shall be due in payable within two weeks of notice of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Counsel to the prevailing party shall submit a copy of the proposed order prior to the hearing via email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com =(302/JPT)
